www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 22 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 22 --

WRITING KEYS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 22 --

Simple, Short, Short
• Spoken Word (write like you talk)
• Simple words
• Short, choppy sentences
• Short paragraphs
• Flesch-Kincaid Readability Test (<8)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 22 --

WORD CHOICE
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 22 --

“The 	right 	word 	is 	the 	difference 	between
lightning 	and 	lightning 	bug.”
- 	Mark 	Twain
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 22 --

YOU, YOURS, YOU WILL
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 22 --

GOOD COPY IS DRIVEN BY
GOOD VERBS!
WEAK COPY 	IS DRIVEN BY
ADJECTIVES & ADVERBS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 22 --

Weak Verbs!
• 	Weak verbs are vague, fuzzy, static
• 	Have
• 	Do
• 	Get
• 	Show
• 	Show
• 	Use
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 22 --

Good Verbs!
• Good verbs appeal to one or more senses
• “Good verbs invite us to see, hear, touch, taste,
smell objects and ideas.”
• Good verbs convey visual imagery and action
• Good verbs demonstrate motion and activity
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 22 --

Good Verbs!
• Replace adverbs with better singular verbs
• Slowly walk -> Strut
• Gently touch -> Caress
• Loudly yell -> Scream
• Vigorously push -> Shove
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 22 --

Present-Tense Verbs
• Grew -> Growing
• Scaled -> Scaling
• Drove -> Driving
• Got - > Getting
• Saved -> Saving
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 22 --

Pluralizing The Verb
• Put music in your life
• Puts music in your life
• Burn ten pounds of fat
• Burns ten pounds of fat
• Get more clicks
• Gets you more clicks
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 22 --

Adjectives
• 	Only use adjectives when they add more information
• 	Avoid fluff adjectives, meaningless (e.g. amazing,
incredible, fantastic, superb, outstanding, great, excellent,
extraordinary, awesome, top-shelf, supreme, striking)
• 	Choose better singular adjectives
• 	Really giving -> Generous
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 22 --

Evocative, 	Not 	Emotive
• Don’t emote; evoke!
• About stimulating their emotions, not sharing yours
• “I’m so excited to tell you…” vs. “You’ll be excited when
you hear…”
• Pair evocative, sensory (experiential) language with
the pain
• “No more sitting in silence, so no more feeling anxious
on calls.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 22 --

“And” vs. “But”
• Be careful: “But” negates what was said prior
• “This is super simple to use. But, it’s also extremely
powerful.”
• “This is super simple to use. And, it’s also extremely
powerful.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 22 --

“If” vs. “When”
• Replace “If” statements with “When” statements
• If you're struggling to get…
• When you're struggling to get…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 22 --

The word “that”…
• 	Trim all the unnecessary uses of the word “that”
• 	“He said that he was going to the store so that he could
get some food.
• 	“He said he was going to the store so he could get some
food.”
• 	Replace the word “that” with “which”
• 	You can do more. And that means that you get faster
results.
• 	You can do more. Which means you get faster results.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 22 --

Emotionally-Charged Words
• 	Embarrass -> Humiliate
• 	Take -> Swipe
• 	Beat -> Pummeled
• 	Won -> Dominated
• 	Tired -> Exhausted
• 	Made fun of -> Mocked
• 	Broke -> Crushed
• 	Told -> Snitched
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 22 --

Loaded Language
• 	A word or phrase is "loaded" when it has a secondary,
evaluative meaning in addition to its primary, descriptive
meaning.
• 	Pro-Abortion vs. Pro-Choice
• 	Weed vs plant
• 	Pest vs Insect
• 	Vaccine vs. Medical Procedure
• 	“Her benefits were slashed” vs. “Her benefits were reduced"
• 	“The 	Trump wall” vs. “The wall approved by Congress in 2006”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 22 --

METAPHOR
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 22 --
